A strong fight will be made In
the commission that is drafting a
new city charter against the four
year term of office that was In a
measure agreed upon by tine char,
ter commission at its last meeting.
A sentiment has developed against
the four-year term and in favor OF
the two-year period, which means
a more frequent submission of men
and municipal questions to the
people.


There will also be an effort
made to get. into the charter all
matters pertaining to the issuance
of public service franchises in
stead of leaving this matter to be
juggled by subsequent city commits
sioners, as such things have been
in the past. The, idea is to elabo-
rate in detail the terms and condi-
tions under which every franchise
may be issued. with the right OF
the purchase of the utility by the
people at designated period
